sentences.text: She came to Brunnlitz, and she got scarlet fever.
I went out, and I stole a-- a scarf, you know, I got hit from the sturmfuhrer.
The girl what is taking care-- the German girl.
I had saw this-- I should put on some-- she should put on something, a scarf, woolen scarf I had.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And when you-- we didn't have [? where ?] to steal, only sometime when they are going to the kitchen I find out this-- find on the floor a cabbage.
And when everybody was asleep, I-- I gave everyone, I gave a leaf of cabbage.
How many was in that neighborhood, how much I had.
On my head, on my back, with a [NON-ENGLISH]
I don't know this has, you hit like this.
Schindler came and pick us up.
They were talking about he came, and he probably gave money.
And he took us to-- to the factory.
Leaving Auschwitz, you went-- everybody, when we came to Auschwitz, we went-- went in-- like cows in a wagon.
When we came to Auschwitz, and when we went.
We have to-- we went to Auschwitz or to Czechoslovakia.
To the factory, to the barrack we got there, all of us.
We'd look for perches where we're going to sleep.
In a room, in a big room.
And she was sick for a long time.
They took her to the hospital.
They take of here, because she get well.
This was just for taking time.
Just for the time when-- when we-- we got out.
It was the plan they're gonna kill us there in Auschwitz, in Czechoslovakia.
A mass grave they make already.
And they tell us, somebody tell me, from the office.
This was a lagerfuhrer a German.
[PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] But I think Schindler took care of this.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Banker, Schindler without banker, banker tell him everything what to do.
I-- I know her for a long time.
But I don't-- I don't-- I don't know nothing.
She had a-- he had a girlfriend, Paula.
And he wrote to the banker, he should take of-- banker should take of his girlfriend.
But banker couldn't-- couldn't help-- he couldn't do nothing.
I hear they talk about Mrs.
Sturmbann girls, but nothing-- just I went to the toilet, and it was sunny beautiful.
So this sun, I wanted, I ask her, can I stay here for a while?
She said, no sun is for Jews.
Waiting for the toilet in line.
You're not gonna have any sun anymore.
They say not long, not long.
One German was walking with me.
No he was-- he was ahead.
The Russian came, and they were talking and talking, till they get-- we get the soup.
And they talk-- there were all this talking a lot at first.
I didn't remember-- and I didn't understand.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I was-- for transport I was waiting, we should go back to Poland.
So I went-- I went to [?
I must tell you, the people there were wonderful.
They give us, nuns, they give us rooms in the-- in the places, and they give us food five times a week-- a day.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] They were so nice to us.
No, I was not away-- aware.
They have nothing-- no contact with me.
I have a card I am going from concentration camp.
It was people waiting for us.
They took us to-- which to the nuns.
And they give me a-- a room-- not a room, a lot of people together.
In a convent, the nuns-- nuns they were so nice.
I wanted to go to Krakow.
I wanted to see who's alive, and who's-- so I couldn't wait to go.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] We went to Rabka.
And I hitchhike a German car, with my sister.
And they took us to Rabka.
Yes, to our apartment I go.
I don't know what happened to my sister, Necha.
Yes, all this news, they killed my sister's boyfriend.
He went out for the paper.
And they kill-- who else did they kill?
Yeah, [INAUDIBLE], a girl, she was walking there.
They, after work, they tell her to stay.
And she was a sickly girl.
Next thing we know, she-- he was-- he killed her.
The landlord, what-- who was our landlord, he-- I find out-- he kill-- he sent to the gestapo my mother.
My mother sent to-- my-- my sister to him, she was ask him, she wanted to ask him, I left our apartment everything.
I left-- he should give us some money for bread.
He took my sister, and put-- send her to-- to gestapo.
Now-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] he sent her to gestapo, and the-- the gestapo killed her.
They said, she had to sign a paper, she was a-- a Russian spy.
She didn't know nothing about anything.
She just was reading and studying.
[PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] They killed her, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] so beautiful she was, so beautiful, blonde.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Later, my mother came to look for my sister.
He took my mother, and he took her to-- sent her to-- to gestapo.
Through my friends, I meet my husband.
We went to Germany, and I got married.
I went to Regensburg, and Regensburg is when I went to Konstanz.
He was from Peterswaldau He was from German town, in-- yes.
My sister's room, a rabbi, Levertov, was in our-- in our-- in our camp.
And I was married by a rabbi.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] He was banker's friend.
Not long, right away after I got married, I ran away from Krakow.
I didn't want to stay there.
My sister was married to banker.
Before I came, she got married to banker.
[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And I didn't have no home.
So I went-- they have a quota.
They say if you come in time, you can take care of things.
I went by car, color people, took me to-- to Konstanz not to Konstanz to Regensburg, Germany-- the German zone.
We traveled-- we're hiding at night.
And the English people took us.
In this truck, with color people.
[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Regensburg, yeah, in Germany.
Not long, maybe a week or two.
And we met a boy, a young boy.
It's a Swiss-- it's not far from Switzerland.
And I was working in Switzerland in a factory.
In Konstanz, he was a gardener.
He learned to be a gardener.
No, I didn't have a home.
I have a room by a German girl, a woman.
Maybe-- I was pregnant, and I got a baby there, in Konstanz.
Is first thing, [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Mary Ann was born.
She was drinking milk and eating crackers.

I think this was for soldiers.
I was-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I was thinking here is-- it's not anti-Semitism, it's not this, I was thinking.
I was very happy, but-- this no-- I was thinking there's not anti-Semitism.
And she was very nice, very helpful.
She was American, from the Joint.
Downtown, I took-- I found an apartment for $18.
Jefferson Street in New York, downtown.
The mouse were running like crazy in the house.

In Germany, I lived-- in Konstanz, I lived in like a nice-- nice apartment I have.
And here I was thinking it's going to be beautiful.
It's going to be-- it was very tough [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] till I go out from there.
I move to-- here in Jackson Heights.
And now you know where I was.
Not much, and I was very nervous, very-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] maybe they asked me.
I have no contact with nobody.
